conflictOfInterest.details: No financial, employment, source-ownership, or personal conflict was identified. github:VSBDev is also the accountable authoring principal; this was a fresh role-isolated run with a distinct run ID as permitted during the founding stage, and its independence is disclosed and attested rather than independently verified.

evidenceChecked: Exact in-memory pack render matched site/data/lessons/PREM-LPP-006.json: ten scenes and four assessment items; the pack tree contains no figures, diagrams, molecule files, or media assets.

Chromium source/render audit at 1280px and 390px: manifest-order headings, five semantic tables, four labelled forms, keyboard submit/retry, 3px visible focus, no global overflow, reduced-motion path, print parity, and zero runtime errors.
Live 2026-07-20 checks of the five declared NLM/NCBI source pages, NLM/NCBI copyright and usage policies, and applicable robots.txt paths; no reviewed source path was disallowed or marked human-only.

Source-expression comparison across lesson prose, examples, questions, tables, glossary, and assessment versus the declared NLM/NCBI pages; no quotation, close source-following structure, adapted media, or dataset extraction was found.

Whole-pack source/security inspection for raw HTML, scripts, event handlers, remote embeds, unsafe TeX, traversal, symlinks, secrets, identifiable learner or patient data, clinical directions, and copyrighted answer-bank material.

findings.finding: Equivalent access is provided for the core outcome. All learning relationships are expressed in prose, code-like linear concept blocks, or headered tables; no visual, audio, color, position, motion, timing, drag, or physical action carries unique meaning. The rendered manifest order exposes one meaningful level-one heading per scene, labelled native controls, keyboard-operable submit and retry actions, programmatic feedback, visible focus, stable 390px reflow, reduced-motion behavior, and complete print content. This supports blind, low-vision, keyboard-only, motor-impaired, narrow-screen, and print learners without lowering the assessed information-literacy operation.
findings.resolution: No change required for this candidate.

findings.finding: The rights ledger traces five official NLM/NCBI webpages to facts-only use, concrete rights evidence, dated access checks, and public terms locations. Live policy and robots checks were consistent with the records. The lesson's decision chain, fictional cases, tables, practice, assessment, and wording form an independent instructional synthesis rather than a source-following paraphrase. The pack declares no third-party assets and contains no quotation, adapted expression, redistributed media, or dataset extraction. Original lesson content is offered under CC BY 4.0, while source material and project identity remain excluded as stated, preserving defensible provenance for rights holders and downstream reusers.
findings.resolution: No change required for this candidate.

findings.finding: The candidate is inert, local source data: no raw HTML, JavaScript, active SVG, executable notebook, unsafe TeX, event handler, remote embed, path traversal, symlink, arbitrary read, secret, personal identifier, patient media, copyrighted answer bank, or unnecessary personal-data request was present. Assessment is untimed and uses fictional non-clinical contexts; boundaries explicitly prohibit diagnosis, treatment selection, and unsupported public-health recommendations. Learners need not disclose health or disability data or access a paid database.
findings.resolution: No change required for this candidate.

findings.finding: The accountable author principal and material authoring agent system, provider, model, version, run ID, instructions digest, assistance scope, and attestation limitation are consistently disclosed across metadata, brief, and attribution. The records do not claim independent verification, review, adjudication, publication, coverage, learner effectiveness, or professional authority. The authoring run ID differs from this review run ID.
findings.resolution: No change required for this candidate.

limitations: This was not a native screen-reader, speech-input, switch-device, mobile-device hardware, or disabled-learner usability session. Accessibility conclusions are based on complete source inspection plus semantic DOM, keyboard, focus, narrow-reflow, reduced-motion, and print checks in headless Chromium.

Automated validation and browser checks cannot prove conformance in every assistive technology, browser, zoom implementation, or future exporter. PDF, EPUB, DOCX, and JATS paths were not tested because the repository identifies those adapters as unavailable.

This policy review is not legal advice. U.S.-government works are not copyright-protected in the United States, but treatment can vary by jurisdiction; the candidate does not depend on reusing their expression because the reviewed uses are facts-only original synthesis.

Live source and terms checks establish only what was observable on 2026-07-20. The author's earlier access sequence and absence of hidden source restrictions are disclosed attestations and cannot be reconstructed cryptographically from the candidate.

Agent system, provider, model, version, run identity, and instruction digests are self-reported provenance checked for internal consistency, not externally verified identity or model-family independence.

Dissent: no dissent arose within this accessibility-and-rights review. Other reviewer conclusions and any adjudication draft were intentionally not inspected, so cross-review agreement or dissent was not assessed.
